---
title: "World Development Indicators (2022)"
subtitle: "Exploratory Analysis of GDP, Inflation, and Growth"
author: "Sarah Shao"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: solarized
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
    background-transition: fade
    center: true
    progress: true
    toc: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  freeze: false
bibliography: references.bib
csl: https://www.zotero.org/styles/apa
---

## Overview

- Data: World Development Indicators (World Bank, 2022) (@worldbank_wdi_2022)
- Year analysed: 2022
- Indicators:
  - GDP per capita
  - Inflation rate
  - GDP growth rate
- Objective: Examine cross-country economic heterogeneity

---

## Load the Dataset

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: true
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/danilofreire/qtm350/main/assignments/wdi.csv"
wdi = pd.read_csv(url)

print("✅ Loaded WDI:", wdi.shape, "(rows, columns)")
wdi.head(5)
```

---

## GDP per Capita Distribution

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
wdi["gdp_per_capita"].dropna().hist(bins=30)
plt.title("Distribution of GDP per Capita (2022)")
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita (current US$)")
plt.ylabel("Number of Countries")
plt.show()
```

## Finding 1

- Highly right-skewed distribution  
- Majority of countries at lower income levels  
- Small number of extreme high-income outliers  

---

## Inflation Distribution

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
wdi["inflation_rate"].dropna().hist(bins=30)
plt.title("Distribution of Inflation Rates (2022)")
plt.xlabel("Inflation Rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("Number of Countries")
plt.show()
```

## Finding 2

- Most countries between 0%–20%  
- Extreme outliers create long right tail  
- Some countries experienced deflation  

---

## GDP Growth Distribution

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
wdi["gdp_growth_rate"].dropna().hist(bins=30)
plt.title("Distribution of GDP Growth Rates (2022)")
plt.xlabel("GDP Growth Rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("Number of Countries")
plt.show()
```

## Finding 3

- Moderate growth (0–10%) for most countries  
- Some negative growth cases  
- One extreme high-growth outlier  

---

## Relationship: GDP per Capita & Inflation

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
plt.scatter(wdi["gdp_per_capita"], wdi["inflation_rate"])
plt.title("GDP per Capita vs Inflation (2022)")
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita (current US$)")
plt.ylabel("Inflation Rate (%)")
plt.show()
```

## Interpretation
- No strong linear relationship  
- Inflation varies across income levels  
- Consistent with macroeconomic research (@fischer1993)

---

## Key Insights

- Strong cross-country heterogeneity  
- Income inequality reflected in skewed GDP distribution  
- Inflation and growth display wide dispersion  
- Outliers strongly influence distributions  

---

## Conclusion

The 2022 WDI data reveal substantial economic variation across countries.

Income levels are highly unequal, and macroeconomic stability differs widely across regions.

These findings highlight the importance of understanding dispersion and structural heterogeneity in global economic analysis.

---

## References
